title: Figure 7 from: Cabarroi-Hernández M, Villalobos-Arámbula AR, Torres-Torres MG, Decock C, Guzmán-Dávalos L (2019) The Ganoderma weberianum-resinaceum lineage: multilocus phylogenetic analysis and morphology confirm G. mexicanum and G. parvulum in the Neotropics. MycoKeys 59: 95-131. https://doi.org/10.3897/mycokeys.59.33182
author: Cabarroi-Hernández, Milay

description: Figure 7 Morphological features and microscopic structures of Ganoderma sessiliformeA–F NY 98713 (holotype) A pilear surface B context not fully homogeneous with discrete bodies of the resin-like deposits (arrow) C–D basidiospores with free to subfree pillars E–F cuticular cells in KOH E cylindrical F clavate, with narrow lumen (arrow) G–K Guzmán 2078, ENCB G pilear surface H–I basidiospores with free to subfree pillars J cuticular cells in Melzer reagent K smooth-walled chlamydospore from context in Melzer reagent.
steps_raw: Figure 7 Morphological features and microscopic structures of Ganoderma sessiliformeA–F NY 98713 (holotype) A pilear surface B context not fully homogeneous with discrete bodies of the resin-like deposits (arrow) C–D basidiospores with free to subfree pillars E–F cuticular cells in KOH E cylindrical F clavate, with narrow lumen (arrow) G–K Guzmán 2078, ENCB G pilear surface H–I basidiospores with free to subfree pillars J cuticular cells in Melzer reagent K smooth-walled chlamydospore from context in Melzer reagent.
